title: Bedroom Parameter Manifest
description: Six knobs an LLM front-end may turn for bedroom.

ceiling_height_m.rationale: Ceiling height is included as a spatial-volume parameter linked to perceived openness and abstract cognition.

daylight_intensity.description: Scalar controlling daylight contribution through windows.
daylight_intensity.rationale: Daylight is included because light exposure can affect restoration, circadian regulation, alertness, and sleep-related comfort.

wall_warmth_index.description: 0 = cooler wall/material palette; 1 = warmer wall/material palette.
wall_warmth_index.rationale: Warm wall and material cues are included because bedroom comfort depends strongly on perceived softness, calmness, and stress reduction.

furniture_fullness_pct.description: Scalar controlling the amount of major furniture in the bedroom.
furniture_fullness_pct.rationale: Furniture fullness is included as a proxy for clutter and crowding, which can increase cognitive load and reduce perceived restfulness.

bed_size_scale.description: Scale factor applied to bed and mattress dimensions.
bed_size_scale.rationale: Bed scale affects the dominant comfort object in the room and changes perceived spaciousness, softness, and restorative affordance.

pillow_count.description: Number of visible pillows placed on the bed.
pillow_count.rationale: Pillow count is included as a visible softness and comfort cue; the Phase 2 survey observed pillow-related generation in the bedroom source.
